user_brief: Create an original square repository logo for an open-source non-commercial project named 'Ansys AEDT MCP Server'. Do not copy or imitate the official Ansys logo or any trademark. Visual concept: abstract electromagnetic field lines, simulation mesh, small connected MCP nodes, and a subtle desktop engineering tool motif. Professional technical style, clean vector-like raster, high contrast, modern GitHub project branding, works at small avatar size, no visible text, transparent background if supported. Use a palette of deep graphite, electric cyan, signal amber, and white highlights.

detected_entities.name: Ansys
detected_entities.why_it_matters: Trademarked engineering software brand; the logo and identity must not be copied or imitated. The project name references Ansys, so the visual language should stay clearly original and avoid brand confusion.

detected_entities.name: AEDT
detected_entities.why_it_matters: Stands for Ansys Electronics Desktop, a simulation platform for electromagnetics/electronics workflows. The logo should evoke electromagnetic simulation and engineering UI motifs without reproducing any official AEDT branding.

detected_entities.name: MCP Server
detected_entities.why_it_matters: Indicates a Model Context Protocol server/tooling project. Visual cues can include small connected nodes, protocol/network structure, and a software-service feel.

detected_entities.name: Electromagnetic simulation
detected_entities.why_it_matters: Defines the core technical theme: field lines, meshes, solver-like geometry, and high-tech visualization language.

detected_entities.name: GitHub repository logo
detected_entities.why_it_matters: Implies small-size readability, square avatar composition, bold silhouette, and limited detail for icon legibility.

search_queries: Ansys Electronics Desktop official product overview site:ansys.com
Ansys Electronics Desktop unified platform electromagnetic simulation site:ansyshelp.ansys.com
Model Context Protocol server visual icon nodes abstract logo

knowledge_notes.point: Ansys Electronics Desktop (AEDT) is described by Ansys as a platform for electronics system design and access to EM solvers such as HFSS, Maxwell, Q3D Extractor, SIwave, and Icepak.
AEDT is characterized in Ansys documentation as a unified environment where electromagnetic, thermal, and circuit designs can be created, simulated, and post-processed.
knowledge_notes.source: Getting Started with Ansys Electronics Desktop (ansyshelp.ansys.com)

knowledge_notes.point: Ansys developer documentation states AEDT supports scripting/automation through ACT Python, PyAEDT, and related libraries.
knowledge_notes.source: Ansys Developer Portal electronics and semiconductors guide (developer.ansys.com)

knowledge_notes.point: Ansys has trademark usage guidelines; official marks should not be copied or imitated in derivative branding.
Ansys broadly positions its products around physics-based simulation and engineering workflows across electronics and multiphysics.

visual_resource_notes.visual_use: Use flowing arcs, vector field lines, contour-like curves, or wavefront rings to communicate EM behavior at a glance.
visual_resource_notes.source: Ansys Electronics product page and AEDT documentation (ansys.com; ansyshelp.ansys.com)

visual_resource_notes.visual_use: Use a sparse triangular or quad mesh overlay, or a faceted geometric core, to imply finite-element simulation.
visual_resource_notes.source: AEDT product positioning and documentation (ansys.com; ansyshelp.ansys.com)

visual_resource_notes.visual_use: Use 3–5 small linked nodes to represent MCP/server connectivity and orchestration.
visual_resource_notes.source: Inferred from the MCP Server concept; no official source required

visual_resource_notes.visual_use: Use a subtle window-frame, panel, or schematic-like UI block shape, but keep it abstract and non-branded.
visual_resource_notes.source: AEDT described as a unified desktop environment (ansyshelp.ansys.com)

visual_resource_notes.visual_use: Use dark base + luminous accent colors so the logo remains readable at small avatar sizes.
visual_resource_notes.source: Inferred best practice for GitHub repository logos

original_reference_notes.canonical_detail: Unified electronics design environment for EM, thermal, and circuit workflows; associated with solver ecosystems like HFSS, Maxwell, Q3D, SIwave, and Icepak.
original_reference_notes.how_to_use_in_prompt: Translate into abstract EM field visual cues, mesh geometry, and technical workflow symbolism; do not copy any UI layout, product icon, or brand mark.
original_reference_notes.source: Ansys Electronics product page and AEDT help docs (ansys.com; ansyshelp.ansys.com)

original_reference_notes.canonical_detail: Official marks are protected and should be used only in approved forms.
original_reference_notes.how_to_use_in_prompt: Add explicit constraints: original iconography only, no logo resemblance, no wordmark, no color/layout echo of official branding.

ambiguities_to_resolve_in_prompt: Whether the icon should feel more like a solver/field-visualization badge or more like a server/network badge.
How literal the desktop engineering motif should be: a faint window outline versus a more abstract panel/grid suggestion.
Whether the mesh should be dense and technical or simplified for stronger avatar legibility.
Whether the MCP nodes should be integrated into the field lines or form a distinct constellation/cluster.

usage_context.reference_policy: use original designs and content as visual reference when they clarify the user's brief

generation_quality_constraints: Keep the silhouette readable at 32x32 and 64x64 avatar sizes.
Avoid all visible text, letters, numbers, or wordmarks.
Do not imitate the official Ansys logo, color arrangement, or branding style too closely.
Use a square composition with strong center-weighted focal point.
Prefer clean vector-like raster edges, minimal clutter, and high contrast.
Ensure the design still reads as an engineering/simulation project when heavily downscaled.
Use a transparent or neutral background with no distracting scenery.
Balance electromagnetic curves, mesh geometry, and node connectivity without overloading the icon.

recommended_visual_direction.format: Square repository avatar/logo, centered emblem, transparent background if supported
recommended_visual_direction.composition: A bold central geometric core wrapped by 2–4 luminous electromagnetic field arcs, with a restrained mesh overlay and 3–5 connected MCP nodes integrated into the perimeter or core. Add a subtle desktop-panel or schematic-window cue as a framing shape, not as a literal UI screenshot.
recommended_visual_direction.style: Original, professional technical branding; clean vector-like raster; slightly futuristic but restrained; high legibility; geometric and minimal; no text
recommended_visual_direction.palette: Deep graphite base, electric cyan primary glow, signal amber secondary accents, white highlight points
must_include: Simulation mesh or faceted solver geometry
must_avoid: Any resemblance to the official Ansys logo
Overly complex circuitry that disappears at avatar size
Soft pastel colors or playful cartoon styling
Brand-confusable layouts or trademark-like color blocking
